text: Prevention of postpartum hemorrhage (PPH) focuses on the active management of the third stage of labor, with oxytocin identified as the gold standard for both prevention and initial treatment [27701915, 36345105].

text: Management strategies emphasize early diagnosis through measured blood loss, hemodynamic resuscitation, and a staged approach using uterotonics, uterine massage, and mechanical interventions like balloon tamponade [36345105, 37832561].
rationale: Claim has cited support in the loaded Evidence Pack.

text: If pharmacological and conservative surgical measures fail, peripartum hysterectomy is considered the definitive life-saving procedure [27701915, 30578144].
rationale: Claim has cited support in the loaded Evidence Pack.

text: To prevent and manage postpartum hemorrhage, healthcare providers use a combination of active management during birth and the use of specific medications to help the uterus contract [27701915, 32575159].
rationale: Claim has cited support in the loaded Evidence Pack.

text: Research also emphasizes the importance of using standardized toolkits and clear protocols to ensure a rapid and coordinated response when heavy bleeding occurs [36345105].
